name: Infisical
license: Open source core / enterprise features review required
what: Secrets management platform for application secrets, machine identity, secret sync, and environment configuration.
solves: Centralizing secrets outside scattered .env files, improving rotation, access control, and auditability across many self-hosted apps.
users: Engineering and platform teams operating multiple services and environments.
fit: Excellent fit. Hylono’s stack is large enough that a real secrets layer is more valuable than ad hoc environment files.
runtime: Web/API platform with encrypted secret storage, auth, access controls, and SDK/CLI/agent integrations.
components: Control plane/UI, database, encryption/key management, CLI/SDK, secret sync/injection, and policy/audit layers.
deploy: Self-host docs are mature; production requires reliable DB, key management, backup, and role design.
deps: Database, encryption keys/KMS strategy, SSO/auth, backup plan, and agent/injection method choices.
datastores: Database for metadata and encrypted secrets.
auth: Local users, SSO/OIDC/SAML depending on plan/setup, machine identities, and scoped access policies.
apis: CLI/SDK/API, secret sync/injection, dynamic access patterns, and audit logs.
security: High leverage and high consequence. Root keys, bootstrap process, admin access, and backup recovery must be handled carefully.
setup_path: Prioritize early if the stack grows. Start with core app/environment secrets, then onboard dependent services in phases.
env: Bootstrap admin, DB settings, encryption keys, base URL, auth provider config, and secret-sync targets.
persistence: Back up database and key material; test disaster recovery because secrets loss or compromise is severe.
operations: Access review, key rotation, secrets rotation, audit review, and onboarding discipline matter more than raw install complexity.
failure_points: Migrating too many apps at once, mishandling root keys, or assuming every app can ingest secrets the same way.
integration: Strong fit with Next.js, n8n, Novu, Kong, databases, and all self-hosted app deployments. Pair well with ZITADEL for SSO.
verify: Decide whether Hylono wants Infisical as the single secrets control plane, and how it coexists with current deployment tooling.
risk: Moderate but worth it; benefits compound as the stack grows.
gdpr: Mostly credential data, but secrets platform security affects every system that holds personal data.
overlap: Could overlap with managed cloud secret stores, but strongest value comes from multi-app self-hosted consistency.
codex_read_first: Read current env/secret handling across the Hylono stack before planning migration.
codex_best_path: Introduce Infisical early for shared infrastructure and integration services, then gradually move app secrets under policy control.
codex_never_assume: Never migrate secrets without a rollback plan and bootstrap key handling procedure.
